steps.title: Isolate the $b$-term
steps.content: Start from $a = \frac{3}{11}b + 6$. Subtract 6 from both sides: $$a - 6 = \frac{3}{11}b$$

steps.title: Solve for $b$
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $\frac{11}{3}$ (the reciprocal of $\frac{3}{11}$): $$b = \frac{11}{3}(a - 6)$$ Distribute: $$b = \frac{11}{3}a - \frac{11}{3} \cdot 6 = \frac{11}{3}a - 22$$

steps.title: Compare to each choice
steps.content:
• A) $b = \frac{3}{11}a + 6$ — just swaps variables, wrong coefficient and sign; eliminate.
  • B) $b = \frac{3}{11}a - 22$ — correct constant but wrong slope ($\frac{3}{11}$ vs $\frac{11}{3}$); eliminate.
  • C) $b = \frac{11}{3}a - 6$ — correct slope but wrong constant ($-6$ vs $-22$); eliminate.
  • D) $b = \frac{11}{3}a - 22$ — matches the derived expression exactly.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
